text:
Sophie loved stories. She spun tales around her knitting Guild. Every sock had its own secret life.

One afternoon, she heard tales from far. An old fox whispered secrets. She liked his tales.

Why do owls do that?

Maybe to scatter dreams. You'd owl fly at night.

She imagined. Maybe dreams don't take flight, but glide. Like snow. Feather soft.

She knit a dream stole.

Stars bright. Moon luna.

She liked owl nights. Then she'd watch snow fall.

Her tales. Owls knew them all.

One chilly October. She fell.

Gossip snow. Winter soft.

Time flew. Owls knew.

She found feathers. Owls wanted. Maybe.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
